title: Amazon Q Developer Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands and techniques for using Amazon Q Developer effectively.

commands.description: Selects the advanced model for complex queries
commands.scenario: When handling complex data queries that require deep analysis

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the model's output
commands.scenario: When generating responses that need to be more deterministic and less creative

commands.description: Enables streaming of responses for real-time applications
commands.scenario: When building a real-time data analysis dashboard

commands.command: Think step by step. Then verify your answer.
commands.description: Encourages the model to use logical reasoning
commands.scenario: When solving complex engineering problems that require step-by-step logic

commands.description: Enables the model to use contextual information for better results
commands.scenario: When integrating with a system that requires understanding of historical data

commands.description: Limits the maximum number of tokens in the response
commands.scenario: When you need concise answers to save on processing costs

commands.description: Provides detailed output for debugging purposes

commands.command: Use examples to clarify your request.
commands.description: Improves model understanding by providing examples
commands.scenario: When the model needs clarification on ambiguous queries

commands.description: Adjusts settings to minimize cost while maintaining performance
commands.scenario: When running large-scale data processing tasks on a budget

commands.description: Activates debug mode for detailed logging
commands.scenario: When needing to trace errors in complex queries

commands.command: Use concise language for better results.
commands.description: Encourages the model to focus on key information
commands.scenario: When you need precise and relevant answers quickly

commands.description: Enables batch processing for handling multiple queries efficiently
commands.scenario: When processing large datasets in parallel to save time

commands.description: Utilizes caching to improve response times for repeated queries
commands.scenario: When frequently querying the same data sets to enhance performance

commands.description: Manually constrain the token window to prioritize specific local files over global repository context to prevent noise.

commands.command: Act as a Senior Site Reliability Engineer (SRE) to review this code for potential race conditions and memory leaks.
commands.description: Role-based persona prompting to shift the model's reasoning from syntax correction to architectural robustness.
Instructs the assistant to output only the changed lines of code rather than the entire file, reducing latency and token usage.

commands.command: Analyze the following code and generate a JUnit/PyTest suite covering edge cases and boundary conditions.
commands.description: A specific pattern for automated test generation that forces the model to look for failure modes rather than happy paths.
Enables detailed timing metrics for each reasoning step, useful for identifying bottlenecks in complex multi-turn prompts.
